translations.idiomatic: The purge reached the eunuchs Hou Lan of Fangdong and Ju Yuan of Dongwuyang, both of whom were cashiered.

sentences.zh: 朝廷莫不稱之。

translations.literal: The court none did not praise it.
translations.idiomatic: The whole court applauded.

sentences.zh: 注[一]蔡質《漢儀》曰：「延熹中，京師遊俠有盜發順帝陵，賣御物於市，市長追捕不得。

translations.literal: Comment [1] Cai Zhi Han Ceremonial says: "In Yanxi, roaming braves in the capital dug open Emperor Shun's tomb and sold imperial goods in the market; the market chief could not catch them.
translations.idiomatic: Note [1]: Cai Zhi records that in Yanxi tomb robbers sold imperial grave goods in Luoyang until Zhou Jing summoned Metropolitan Commandant Zuo Xiong to the terrace and forced a confession within three days.

sentences.zh: 周景以尺一詔召司隸校尉左雄詣台對詰，雄伏於廷荅對，景使虎賁左駿頓頭，血出覆面，與三日期，賊便擒也。」

translations.literal: Zhou Jing with the foot-rule edict summoned metropolitan commandant Zuo Xiong to the terrace for questioning; Xiong prostrated in the courtyard answering; Jing had a tiger groom from the left strike his head until blood covered his face, gave three days' limit, and the thieves were captured."
translations.idiomatic: He had guards beat Zuo Xiong until he bled, then gave him three days to make the arrests—which were done."

sentences.zh: 長子崇嗣，至甘陵相。

translations.literal: Eldest son Chong succeeded; he reached chancellor of Ganling.
translations.idiomatic: His eldest son Zhou Chong inherited the title and became chancellor to the king of Ganling.
Editorial note [1].

sentences.zh: 注[一]甘陵王理相也。

translations.literal: Comment [1] Chancellor to King Li of Ganling.
translations.idiomatic: Note [1]: He was chancellor to Liu Li, king of Ganling.

sentences.zh: 理即章帝曾孫。

translations.literal: Li is Emperor Zhang's great-grandson.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Li was a great-grandson of Emperor Zhang.

sentences.zh: 景子忠

translations.literal: Jing's son Zhong.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Zhong, son of Zhou Jing.

sentences.zh: 中子忠，少歷列位，累遷大司農。

translations.literal: Middle son Zhong, in youth passed through successive posts, cumulative promotion to grand minister of agriculture.
translations.idiomatic: The second son, Zhou Zhong, rose through many offices to grand minister of agriculture.

sentences.zh: [一]忠子暉，前為洛陽令，去官歸。

translations.literal: [1] Zhong's son Hui, formerly was magistrate of Luoyang, left office and returned.
translations.idiomatic: [1] His son Zhou Hui had been magistrate of Luoyang and then retired home.

sentences.zh: 兄弟好賓客，雄江淮閒，出入從車常百餘乘。

translations.literal: The brothers loved hosting guests; they were mighty between the Yangzi and Huai; going out and entering their carriages were always more than a hundred teams.
translations.idiomatic: The brothers kept open house between the Yangzi and Huai and traveled with a hundred-car retinue.

sentences.zh: 及帝崩，暉聞京師不安，來候忠，董卓聞而惡之，使兵劫殺其兄弟。

translations.literal: When the emperor died, Hui heard the capital was unsettled and came to visit Zhong; Dong Zhuo heard and hated it, sent troops to seize and kill the brothers.
translations.idiomatic: When the emperor died Zhou Hui came to consult his father; Dong Zhuo had both brothers murdered by soldiers.

sentences.zh: 忠後代皇甫嵩為太尉，錄尚書事，以災異免。

translations.literal: Zhong later replaced Huangfu Song as grand commandant, recorded masters affairs, was dismissed for portents.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Zhong later succeeded Huangfu Song as grand commandant with control of the masters office, then lost the post over omens.

sentences.zh: 復為衛尉，從獻帝東歸洛陽。

translations.literal: Again he was commandant of the guards and followed Emperor Xian east returning to Luoyang.
translations.idiomatic: He served again as commandant of the guards and escorted Emperor Xian back east to Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 注[一]吳書曰，忠字嘉謀，與朱儁共敗李傕於曹陽也。

translations.literal: Comment [1] Wu Records says Zhong's courtesy name was Jiamou; together with Zhu Jun he defeated Li Jue at Caoyang.
translations.idiomatic: Note [1]: The Wu Records gives his style as Jiamou and credits him with Zhu Jun's victory over Li Jue at Caoyang.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：袁公持重，誠單所奉。

translations.literal: Appraisal says: Lord Yuan was steady and grave; sincerity was what he single-mindedly upheld.
translations.idiomatic: The eulogy reads: Yuan An was steadfast; he gave the throne his whole heart.

sentences.zh: [一]惟德不忘，延世承寵。

translations.literal: [1] Only virtue is not forgotten; extending generations he received favor.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Virtue is long remembered, and his line enjoyed favor for generations.

sentences.zh: 孟侯經博，侍言帝幙。

translations.literal: Meng-hou's classics were broad; he attended speech at the emperor's curtain.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Hao mastered the canon and counseled the sovereign at close range.

sentences.zh: 稜﹑榮事君，志同鸇雀。

translations.literal: Leng and Rong served their lords; their intent matched the falcon and sparrow.
translations.idiomatic: Han Leng and Zhou Rong served like hawks striking every breach of duty against their lords.
Editorial note [2].

sentences.zh: 注[一]單，盡也。

translations.literal: Comment [1] Dan means exhaust.
translations.idiomatic: Note [1]: Here the word means utterly or to the utmost.

sentences.zh: 注[二]《左傳》曰：「見無禮於其君者誅之，如鷹鸇之逐鳥雀也。」

translations.literal: Comment [2] Zuo Tradition says: "When one sees lack of ritual toward his lord, execute him—like the hawk and falcon chasing birds and sparrows."
translations.idiomatic: Note [2]: The Zuo Tradition compares punishing disloyalty to a hawk taking small birds.

sentences.zh: 校勘記

translations.literal: Collation notes.
translations.idiomatic: Textual collation notes follow.

sentences.zh: 一五一七頁三行汝南汝陽人也按：集解引惠棟說，謂袁紀作「汝南宛人」。

translations.literal: Page 1517 line 3 "a man of Runan Ruyang"—note: Collected Explications cites Hui Dong that Yuan Ji reads "a man of Runan Wan."
translations.idiomatic: Collation: some editions read Wan instead of Ruyang for Yuan An's birthplace.

sentences.zh: 一五一八頁二行洛陽令身出案行按：殿本考證引孫□說，謂「洛陽」當作「汝陽」。

translations.literal: Page 1518 line 2 "the magistrate of Luoyang went out in person to inspect"—note: Palace edition textual investigation cites Sun □ that "Luoyang" should read "Ruyang."
translations.idiomatic: Collation: an editor argues the magistrate should be of Ruyang, not Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 又按：汲本、殿本「身」作「自」。

translations.literal: Also note: Ji edition and palace edition write zi for shen.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: some editions read zi for shen in this line.

sentences.zh: 一五二０頁四行南陽太守滿殷按：汲本「滿」作「蒲」。

translations.literal: Page 1520 line 4 "grand administrator of Nanyang Man Yin"—note: Ji edition writes 蒲 for 滿.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: the surname appears as Pu in some editions.

sentences.zh: 一五二０頁六行左鹿蠡王阿佟按：集解引惠棟說，謂袁紀「阿佟」作「阿修」。

translations.literal: Page 1520 line 6 "Left Luoli king Atong"—note: Collected Explications cites Hui Dong that Yuan Ji writes "Axiu."
translations.idiomatic: Collation: the Xiongnu prince's name is spelled differently in Yuan Ji.

sentences.zh: 又引錢大昭說，謂疑即於除鞬也。

translations.literal: Also cites Qian Dazhao that it is suspected to be the same as Yuchujian.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Qian Dazhao identifies the figure with Yuchujian.

sentences.zh: 「左」當作「右」。

translations.literal: "Left" should read "right."
translations.idiomatic: Collation: the graph for left should read right.

sentences.zh: 一五二０頁一二行至乎章和之初降者十餘萬人按：汲本「乎」作「於」。

translations.literal: Page 1520 line 12 "down to the beginning of Zhanghe"—note: Ji edition writes yu for hu.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: preposition variant yu versus hu in this line.

sentences.zh: 汲本、殿本「十餘萬人」作「十萬餘人」。

translations.literal: Ji edition and palace edition write "more than a hundred thousand people" as "a hundred thousand and more people."
translations.idiomatic: Collation: word order of the numeral phrase differs in palace editions.

sentences.zh: 一五二二頁一二行未蒙顯贈按：「未」原斗「求」，逕據汲本、殿本改正。

translations.literal: Page 1522 line 12 "did not receive manifest posthumous honor"—note: wei was misprinted qiu; corrected per Ji and palace editions.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: a misprint was emended to wei per Ji and palace editions.

sentences.zh: 一五二三頁四行左中郎*[將]*集解引何焯說，謂「左中郎」下當有「將」字。

translations.literal: Page 1523 line 4 "left gentleman general"—Collected Explications cites He Zhuo that the word general should follow left gentleman-of-the-palace.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: He Zhuo argues for inserting general after left gentleman-of-the-palace.

sentences.zh: 又校補引柳從辰說，謂袁紀亦作「左中郎將」，與華嶠書同。

translations.literal: Also textual supplement cites Liu Congchen that Yuan Ji likewise reads "left gentleman general of the household," matching Hua Qiao's book.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Yuan Ji agrees with Hua Qiao on the fuller title.

sentences.zh: 今據補。

translations.literal: Now supplied accordingly.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: the character is supplied in the text.

sentences.zh: 一五二三頁一一行中常侍袁赦按：集解引惠棟說，謂袁紀作「袁朗」，案梁冀傳當作「赦」。

translations.literal: Page 1523 line 11 "palace attendant Yuan She"—note: Collected Explications cites Hui Dong that Yuan Ji reads "Yuan Lang"; case from Liang Ji biography should be She.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: the eunuch's name is disputed between Lang and She.

sentences.zh: 一五二三頁一四行遂誅隗及術兄基等男女二十餘人按：沈家本謂袁紹傳注引獻帝春秋曰：「卓使司隸宣璠盡口收之，母及姊妹嬰孩以上五十餘人下獄死。

translations.literal: Page 1523 line 14 "thereupon executed Wei and Shu's elder brother Ji and more than twenty men and women"—note: Shen Jiaben says Yuan Shao commentary citing Xiandi Chunqiu has Director of the Metropolitan Region Xuan Fan seize them by edict, mothers and sisters and infants upward fifty-odd persons sent to prison and killed.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: other sources give fifty victims, not twenty; the numeral may be wrong.

sentences.zh: 」獻紀注引亦同。

translations.literal: " Xiandi annals commentary cites the same.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: the same figure appears in the Xiandi commentary.

sentences.zh: 此傳雲二十餘人，恐「二」字誤也。

translations.literal: This biography says more than twenty persons; probably the character "two" is mistaken.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: "twenty" may be a scribal error for "fifty."

sentences.zh: 一五二四頁九行識其狀貌傷其眼目按：汲本、殿本二「其」字皆作「臣」。

translations.literal: Page 1524 line 9 "recognized his appearance pitied his eyes"—note: Ji and palace editions both write 臣 for 其.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: some editions read "my appearance" for "his appearance."

sentences.zh: 一五二六頁一五行徒步師門按：汲本「師門」下有「從師」二字。

translations.literal: Page 1526 line 15 "walked on foot to teacher's gate"—note: Ji edition has two characters 從師 below 師門.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Ji edition adds "to follow a teacher."

sentences.zh: 殿本無「從師」二字，考證雲從宋本刪。

translations.literal: Palace edition lacks 從師; textual investigation says deleted following Song edition.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: palace text omits the phrase per Song recension.

sentences.zh: 一五二七頁一行其從父逢為太尉按：張森楷校勘記謂案袁逢以太僕為司空，未嘗為太尉，「尉」字疑誤，否則竟謝承謬也。

translations.literal: Page 1527 line 1 "his uncle by the father's line Feng was grand commandant"—note: Zhang Senkai collation says Yuan Feng was grand coachman to minister of works, never grand commandant; 尉 may be wrong or Xie Cheng mistaken.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Yuan Feng's highest office may be misstated as grand commandant.

sentences.zh: 一五二七頁一行不聽*

translations.literal: Page 1527 line 1 "would not listen*
translations.idiomatic: Collation: lacuna in the phrase "would not listen."

translations.literal: (Text reads hu summon.)
translations.idiomatic: Collation: some manuscripts insert the verb summon here.

sentences.zh: **[音]*聲而退據汲本、殿本改。

translations.literal: **[sound]* withdrew"—emended per Ji and palace editions.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: the corrupted phrase emended from Ji and palace texts.

sentences.zh: 一五二七頁四行詔秘等門閭號曰七賢按：御覽一五七引作「詔復秘等閭號曰七賢閭」。

translations.literal: Page 1527 line 4 "edict on Mi and others' gate lanes titled Seven Worthies"—note: Imperial Readings 157 cites "edict restored Mi and others' lane title Seven Worthies Lane."
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Yiwen leiju quotes a slightly longer wording.

sentences.zh: 一五二八頁三行曾孫賴吉得立按：刊誤謂案前書「立」當作「全」。

translations.literal: Page 1528 line 3 "the great-grandson relied on Ji and was established"—note: Kanwu says examining Former Documents li should read quan.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Kanwu argues the graph li should read quan by parallel to the Former Han text.

sentences.zh: 一五三一頁三行足以驚百按：汲本「驚」作「警」。

translations.literal: Page 1531 line 3 "suffices to frighten a hundred"—note: Ji edition writes the alarm graph for the startle graph.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: some editions use the graph for warn instead of the graph for startle.

sentences.zh: 一五三二頁九行公又曰宥之及三宥不對走出按：刊誤謂案今禮記文，注多下「公又曰宥之」五字。

translations.literal: Page 1532 line 9 "the duke again said pardon him; when three pardons he did not answer and ran out"—note: Kanwu says present Rites text inserts five more characters "the duke again said pardon him" below.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Kanwu notes a longer Rites quotation in modern texts.

sentences.zh: 一五三六頁七行稜孫演按：桓帝紀「演」作「演」。

translations.literal: Page 1536 line 7 "Leng's grandson Yan"—note: Emperor Huan annals writes the name with a different graph.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: the grandson's name varies in parallel texts.

sentences.zh: 沈欽韓謂胡廣傳作「演」。

translations.literal: Shen Qinhan says Hu Guang biography writes 演.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Hu Guang's biography uses one spelling.

sentences.zh: 李慈銘謂吳志周瑜傳注引張璠漢紀作「演」，與桓紀同。

translations.literal: Li Ciming says Wu chronicles Zhou Yu commentary citing Zhang Fan Hanji writes 演, matching Huan annals.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Zhang Fan's Hanji agrees with the Huan annals spelling.

sentences.zh: 一五三八頁九行豈可令□積一門按：「□」原作「偏」，逕據汲本、殿本改。

translations.literal: Page 1538 line 9 "how can we let partial favor pile in one gate"—note: the lacuna originally read partial; emended per Ji and palace editions.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: the partisan wording was emended to match Ji and palace texts.

sentences.zh: 一五三八頁一三行中常侍防東侯覽宸翰樓覆宋本東漢書刊誤云：「案覽本傳，覽防東人，封高鄉侯。

translations.literal: Page 1538 line 13 "palace attendant Fangdong marquis Lan"—Hanlin tower overlay on Song edition collation error says: examining Lan's biography, Lan was a man of Fangdong, enfeoffed marquis of Gaoxiang.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Hou Lan's fief should read Gaoxiang, not Fangdong, per his biography.

sentences.zh: 今此載其侯爵，當雲高鄉侯，若載其本縣名，則非例也。

translations.literal: Now this records his marquis rank; it ought to say Gaoxiang marquis; if recording his native county it is not the pattern.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: the entry should list his noble title, not his birthplace.

sentences.zh: 蓋脫一『侯』字，誤二『高鄉』字。

translations.literal: Probably one marquis character is missing and two Gaoxiang characters are wrong.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: editors note a missing marquis character and a duplicated place-name phrase.

sentences.zh: 」今按劉氏之意，蓋謂「防東」二字乃「高鄉」之誤，其下又脫一「侯」字。

translations.literal: " Now following Liu's intent, probably the two characters Fangdong are mistaken for Gaoxiang, and below one 侯 is missing.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Liu concludes Fangdong is a corruption for Gaoxiang marquis.

sentences.zh: 是劉氏所見本，亦作「中常侍防東侯覽」也。

translations.literal: The edition that Liu (Ban) saw likewise wrote "palace attendant marquis of Fangdong Lan."
translations.idiomatic: The manuscript Liu Ban collated likewise read "eunuch marquis of Fangdong, Hou Lan."

sentences.zh: 殿本正文作「中常侍防東陽侯侯覽」*

translations.literal: The palace edition main text writes "palace attendant Fang Dongyang marquis Hou Lan"*
translations.idiomatic: The palace block text reads "eunuch, Marquis of Fangdongyang, Hou Lan," with an extra graph in the placename.

sentences.zh: (汲本同)

translations.literal: (Ji edition same)
translations.idiomatic: (The Ji recension agrees.)

sentences.zh: *，而引劉攽刊誤，則刪去「脫一侯字」四字，遂使讀者不知劉氏所言謂何，當時校勘之粗疏如是。

translations.literal: *, yet when citing Liu Ban's collation error it deletes the four characters "missing one hou character," thereupon making readers not know what Liu's words referred to—such was the crudity of collation at the time.
translations.idiomatic: The editors then dropped Liu Ban's remark about a missing marquis character, leaving later readers puzzled—a sloppy piece of textual work.

sentences.zh: 又集解引錢大昕說，謂劉據覽傳證此文當為「高鄉」之誤，是矣。

translations.literal: Also Collected Explications cites Qian Daxin's view, saying Liu's evidence from Lan's biography proves this wording ought to be error for "Gaoxiang"—that is correct.
translations.idiomatic: Collected Explications quotes Qian Daxin: Hou Lan's biography shows the toponym should be Gaoxiang, not Fangdong—and Qian is right.

sentences.zh: 予又疑高鄉即防東之鄉，故傳稱防東鄉侯，因下文有「東武陽」字，又誤「鄉」為「陽」也。

translations.literal: I also suspect Gaoxiang is Fangdong's xiang; therefore the biography calls Fangdong xiang marquis; because below there is "Dongwuyang," they also mistakenly changed xiang to yang.
translations.idiomatic: Gaoxiang may be the district attached to Fangdong, which would explain a title "marquis of Fangdong district"; copyists may have corrupted district into the following placename Yang.

sentences.zh: 今按錢氏之意，蓋謂疑當作「中常侍防東鄉侯侯覽」也。

translations.literal: Now following Qian's intent, probably it ought to read "palace attendant Fangdong xiang marquis Hou Lan."
translations.idiomatic: On Qian Daxin's reading the line should read "eunuch, marquis of Fangdong district, Hou Lan."
